---
title: "Somalogic Batch Analysis"
author: "Dylan Hirsch"
date: "3/30/2019"
output: github_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
eset = readRDS('../../Data/Somalogic/data_analysis_ready/analysis_ready_subject_level_training_somalogic.rds')
library(ggplot2)
```

## XCGD

```{r}
features = c('Chitotriosidase.1', 'PARC', 'somal')
for(feature in features) {
  eset.healthy = eset[,eset$condition == 'Healthy']
  X = t(exprs(eset.healthy))
  df = data.frame(feature = X[, feature], batch = eset.healthy$assay_desc)
  p = ggplot(df, aes(x = batch, y = feature)) + geom_boxplot() + geom_jitter() + ggtitle(feature)
  print(p)
}
```

```{r}
condition = 'XCGD'
eset.condition = eset[, eset$condition == condition]
table(eset.condition$assay_desc)
```
